text:
# What is Bullpen?

Bullpen is a non-custodial trading terminal for prediction markets and onchain trading. It brings Polymarket, Hyperliquid, and Jupiter Ultra into one product, then layers in smart money discovery, copy trading, and a CLI.

## Core Principles

**Self-Custody**: Bullpen never takes custody of your funds. You trade directly from your own wallet using embedded wallets or linked external wallets.

**Best Execution**: Prediction markets execute on Polymarket's CLOB. Perps execute on Hyperliquid. Spot routes through Jupiter Ultra for Solana tokens and through Hyperliquid for listed spot assets.

**Unified Experience**: One interface for everything. No switching between apps to trade different markets or chains.

## Supported Markets

| Market             | Execution Venue    | Assets                                        |
| ------------------ | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------- |
| Prediction Markets | Polymarket CLOB    | All Polymarket events                         |
| Perpetuals         | Hyperliquid        | All HL perp markets                           |
| Spot (Major)       | Hyperliquid / UNIT | BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, and other HL-listed spot |
| Spot (Long-tail)   | Jupiter Ultra      | Any Solana SPL token                          |

## Smart Money & Copy Trading

**Copy Trading**: Mirror positions from profitable Polymarket wallets with automated execution.

**Smart Money Discovery**: Surface wallets with strong P\&L, win rate, and consistency across Polymarket.

**Convergence Signals**: Flags when multiple tracked wallets independently take the same side of a market.

**Alerts**: Real-time notifications when tracked wallets trade above configurable thresholds.

Learn more:

* [Copy Trading](/smart-money/copy-trading.md)
* [Smart Money Discovery](/smart-money/smart-money-discovery.md)
* [Convergence Signals](/smart-money/convergence-signals.md)
* [Smart Money Alerts](/smart-money/smart-money-alerts.md)

## Bullpen CLI

Bullpen CLI lets you discover markets, trade, track portfolios, and consume smart money data from the terminal. It also ships with AI agent skills for Claude Code and Codex CLI.

* [CLI Quickstart](/bullpen-cli/overview.md)

## Who Builds Bullpen?

Bullpen is built by a team with experience across Hyperliquid, Galaxy, Deribit, Paxos, and Kraken. Co-founded by [Ansem](https://twitter.com/blknoiz06).

For more on the team, see [Team](/about-bullpen/what-is-bullpen/team.md).

# Platform Architecture

Bullpen routes trades to different execution venues based on the asset type and market. This page explains how orders flow through the system.

## Trade Routing Overview

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                 BULLPEN                                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                               │
│  ┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐   ┌───────────────┐   ┌─────────────┐            │
│  │  Perps  │   │  Spot   │   │     Spot      │   │ Predictions │            │
│  │         │   │ (Major) │   │  (Long-tail)  │   │             │            │
│  └────┬────┘   └────┬────┘   └───────┬───────┘   └──────┬──────┘            │
│       │             │                │                  │                    │
└───────┼─────────────┼────────────────┼──────────────────┼────────────────────┘
        │             │                │                  │
        ▼             ▼                ▼                  ▼
  ┌───────────┐  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐      ┌────────────┐
  │Hyperliquid│  │   UNIT   │   │ Jupiter  │      │ Polymarket │
  │   Perps   │  │          │   │  Ultra   │      │            │
  └───────────┘  └──────────┘   └──────────┘      └────────────┘
```

## Prediction Markets

All prediction market orders execute through **Polymarket's Central Limit Order Book (CLOB)**. Bullpen connects directly to Polymarket for market discovery, pricing, order placement, and settlement.

Collateral is USDC.e on Polygon. Orders match via price-time priority. Winning positions pay $1.00 per share at resolution and can be redeemed gaslessly.

Bullpen adds additional tooling on top of Polymarket execution: smart money discovery, copy trading, convergence signals, trade feeds, and alerts.

## Perpetuals

All perpetual futures orders execute on **Hyperliquid**.

Hyperliquid operates a fully onchain central limit order book (CLOB) via HyperCore. Every order, cancel, trade, and liquidation happens transparently onchain with block-level finality. Bullpen supports all Hyperliquid perp markets and order types.

Bullpen also supports **HIP-3** (Hyperliquid Improvement Proposal 3), which enables permissionless liquidity provision through a hyperliquidity vault system. This allows for deeper liquidity on long-tail perp markets.

For detailed order type specifications, see [Hyperliquid Docs: Order Types](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/order-types).

## Spot: Major Assets

For spot assets listed on Hyperliquid (BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, MON, XPL, and others), trades route through the **UNIT** integration which accesses Hyperliquid's spot orderbook.

Currently supported UNIT assets include:

| Asset    | Pair          |
| -------- | ------------- |
| HYPE     | HYPE/USDC     |
| BTC      | BTC/USDC      |
| MON      | MON/USDC      |
| SOL      | SOL/USDC      |
| ETH      | ETH/USDC      |
| XPL      | XPL/USDC      |
| USDT     | USDT/USDC     |
| FARTCOIN | FARTCOIN/USDC |
| PUMP     | PUMP/USDC     |

This list expands as Hyperliquid adds new spot markets.

## Spot: Long-Tail Assets

For any Solana SPL token not listed on Hyperliquid, trades execute through **Jupiter Ultra**.

Jupiter Ultra is a meta-aggregation layer that:

* Aggregates quotes from multiple liquidity sources (Iris, JupiterZ, DFlow, Hashflow, OKX)
* Simulates execution quality before routing
* Provides MEV protection via ShadowLane (Jupiter's proprietary transaction landing engine)
* Supports gasless swaps for trades above \~$10 USD

Ultra delivers sub-second transaction landing (50-400ms vs 400ms-1.2s for traditional methods) and consistently achieves positive slippage on average.

For technical details, see [Jupiter Ultra V3 Documentation](https://dev.jup.ag/blog/ultra-v3).

## Account Structure

Bullpen maintains separate balances for different market types:

| Account       | Collateral       | Used For                      |
| ------------- | ---------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Polymarket    | USDC.e (Polygon) | Prediction markets            |
| Perps         | USDC             | Hyperliquid perpetual futures |
| Spot (HL)     | USDC             | Hyperliquid spot markets      |
| Spot (Solana) | SOL / SPL tokens | Solana token trading          |

You can transfer between accounts using the built-in transfer functionality. See [Transfers](/features/transfers.md) for details.

# Creating an Account

### Access the App

Go to [app.bullpen.fi](https://app.bullpen.fi/) on any browser (desktop or mobile).

## Sign-Up Options

Bullpen offers multiple ways to create an account:

| Method          | Description                      |
| --------------- | -------------------------------- |
| **Wallet**      | Connect an existing wallet       |
| **X (Twitter)** | Sign in with your X account      |
| **Google**      | Sign in with your Google account |
| **Email**       | Sign up with email and password  |

Choose whichever method is most convenient for you.

## Embedded Wallet

When you create an account, Bullpen automatically creates a non-custodial embedded wallet using Turnkey. This wallet is used for all trading activity.

* No seed phrase to remember during signup
* Export your private key at any time
* Only you can authorize transactions
* Fully non-custodial

## Importing an Existing Wallet

If you already have a wallet you want to trade with:

1. Create a Bullpen account using any sign-up method above
2. Go to Profile → Wallets
3. Click "Import Wallet"
4. Enter your private key or seed phrase
5. Set the imported wallet as your primary wallet

This lets you trade using an existing wallet while benefiting from Bullpen's interface and execution.

## Next Steps

1. [Fund your wallet](/getting-started/funding-your-wallet.md)
2. [Account Types](/getting-started/account-types.md)
3. [Start trading](/trading/overview.md)

## Security

* Bullpen is non-custodial. Only you control your funds.
* Private keys are secured by your device's hardware security.
* Always verify you're on the official app.bullpen.fi domain.
* Enable device security features (biometrics, strong PIN)

text:
# Funding Your Wallet

## Collateral by Market

| Market            | Required Collateral                |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Polymarket        | USDC.e on Polygon                  |
| Hyperliquid Perps | USDC                               |
| Hyperliquid Spot  | USDC (or the token you're selling) |
| Solana Spot       | SOL + any SPL token                |

## Deposit Methods

| Method                     | Description                                           |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| **Wallet**                 | Connect or paste from any external wallet             |
| **Transfer Crypto**        | Move funds from Coinbase, exchanges, or other wallets |
| **Card**                   | Purchase crypto with a credit or debit card           |
| **Apple Pay / Google Pay** | Quick mobile payments                                 |
| **Bank Transfer**          | ACH or wire for larger deposits                       |

All deposit methods are powered by fun.xyz.

## For Prediction Markets

To trade on Polymarket through Bullpen, you need USDC.e on Polygon:

1. Click **Deposit** in the app
2. Select **USDC on Polymarket**
3. Choose your deposit method
4. Funds arrive in your Polymarket balance

## For Perps Trading

You need USDC in your Hyperliquid perps account:

1. Deposit USDC to Hyperliquid
2. Transfer to your Perps account if needed
3. Ready to trade perps

## For Solana Spot

You need SOL for gas fees, unless you use gasless swaps for trades above \~$10. You also need the token you want to sell, or USDC/SOL if you are buying.

## Minimum Amounts

|               | Minimum                                  |
| ------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Solana gas    | \~0.001 SOL recommended                  |
| Gasless swaps | Available for trades \~$10 USD and above |
| Perps         | No strict minimum                        |

## Transferring Between Accounts

Use the **Transfer** button to move funds between Solana, Hyperliquid Perps, and Hyperliquid Spot.

Transfers within Hyperliquid are instant. Cross-chain transfers take 30 seconds to a few minutes.

See [Transfers](/features/transfers.md) for details.

## Withdrawing Funds

1. Go to your account/wallet section
2. Select "Withdraw"
3. Enter destination address
4. Confirm amount and network
5. Authorize the withdrawal

Withdrawals process immediately. Network confirmation times vary by chain.

## Troubleshooting

**Deposit not showing?** Verify the transaction confirmed on-chain. Cross-chain transfers may take a few minutes.

**Need gas for Solana?** Deposit a small amount of SOL, or use gasless swaps (minimum \~$10 trade).

**Perps account shows 0?** Transfer USDC from your spot or main balance into your perps account.

# Wallet Management

## Your Wallet

When you create a Bullpen account, an embedded wallet is automatically created for you. Access your wallet settings by clicking your profile in the top right corner.

From here you can:

* View your wallet address
* Export your private key
* Import an existing wallet

## Import & Export

### Exporting Your Wallet

To export your private key:

1. Go to Profile > Wallets
2. Select the wallet you want to export
3. Click "Export Private Key"
4. Authenticate to confirm
5. Your private key will be displayed. Copy and store securely

**Security warning:** Never share your private key. Anyone with access to it can control your funds. Store it offline in a secure location.

### Importing an Existing Wallet

If you have an existing wallet you want to use for trading:

1. First create a Bullpen account (if you haven't already)
2. Go to Profile > Wallets
3. Click "Import Wallet"
4. Choose import method:
   * **Private Key**: Paste your existing private key
   * **Seed Phrase**: Enter your 12 or 24-word recovery phrase
5. Label your wallet for easy identification
6. Confirm import

Imported wallets have full functionality. You can trade, transfer, and use them for all Bullpen features.

## Linking a Wallet

You can link an external wallet to your Bullpen account. Linking a wallet provides:

* **Account recovery**: A backup method to recover your account if you lose access
* **Alternative sign-in**: Another way to log into your Bullpen account
* **Future quests**: Proof of wallet ownership for upcoming reward programs

Linking a wallet does not enable trading from that wallet. All trading is done through your embedded Bullpen wallet for optimal speed and user experience.

**Supported wallets:**

Bullpen supports any wallet compatible with Reown (formerly WalletConnect), which includes most major wallets:

* Phantom
* MetaMask
* Coinbase Wallet
* Rainbow
* Trust Wallet
* And many others

**To link a wallet:**

1. Go to Profile > Wallets
2. Click "Link Wallet"
3. Select your wallet or scan the QR code
4. Approve the connection in your wallet

## Best Practices

* Don't share your private keys or seed phrases
* Use a hardware wallet for large holdings
* Regularly verify your wallet addresses before transfers
* Consider using separate wallets for trading vs. long-term storage

# Account Types

Bullpen maintains separate account balances for different market types.

## Account Structure

| Account                | Collateral       | Purpose                           |
| ---------------------- | ---------------- | --------------------------------- |
| **Polymarket**         | USDC.e (Polygon) | Prediction markets                |
| **Perps**              | USDC             | Hyperliquid perpetual futures     |
| **Spot (Hyperliquid)** | USDC             | Hyperliquid spot markets via UNIT |
| **Spot (Solana)**      | SOL / SPL tokens | Solana token trading              |

## Why Separate Accounts?

Different markets have different collateral and settlement requirements:

* **Polymarket** uses USDC.e on Polygon and settles at market resolution
* **Perps** use USDC margin and settle PnL in USDC
* **Hyperliquid spot** settles in the actual tokens traded
* **Solana spot** uses native Solana tokens and SPL standards

Separating accounts ensures collateral for one market type isn't accidentally used for another.

## Viewing Balances

The app shows your balances for each account type:

* **Total Portfolio Value**: Aggregate across all accounts
* **Polymarket Balance**: Available USDC.e + open position value
* **Perps Balance**: Available USDC margin + unrealized PnL
* **Spot Balances**: Token holdings by chain

## Transferring Between Accounts

Use the transfer module to move funds:

1. Open the transfer/account section (bottom-right in the app)
2. Select source account
3. Select destination account
4. Enter amount
5. Confirm transfer

Transfers within Bullpen are near-instant.

### Transfer Routes

| From       | To         | Notes                                   |
| ---------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Solana     | Polymarket | Bridges to USDC.e on Polygon            |
| Solana     | HL Perps   | Transfers SOL/USDC to Hyperliquid       |
| Solana     | HL Spot    | Transfers to Hyperliquid spot account   |
| HL Perps   | HL Spot    | Internal Hyperliquid transfer (instant) |
| HL Spot    | HL Perps   | Internal Hyperliquid transfer (instant) |
| HL Perps   | Polymarket | Bridges USDC to USDC.e on Polygon       |
| HL Spot    | Polymarket | Bridges USDC to USDC.e on Polygon       |
| HL Perps   | Solana     | Transfers back to Solana                |
| Polymarket | Solana     | Bridges USDC.e back to Solana           |
| Polymarket | HL Perps   | Bridges USDC.e to USDC on Hyperliquid   |
| Polymarket | HL Spot    | Bridges USDC.e to USDC on Hyperliquid   |

Cross-chain transfers (Solana ↔ Hyperliquid, Solana ↔ Polymarket, Hyperliquid ↔ Polymarket) may take a few seconds to minutes.

## Account Details

### Polymarket Account

* **Collateral**: USDC.e on Polygon
* **Shows**: Available balance, open position value, unrealized P\&L
* **Used for**: Buying and selling prediction market outcome shares, redeeming winning positions

### Perps Account

* **Collateral:** USDC only
* **Shows:** Balance, unrealized PnL, available margin
* **Used for:** Opening/closing perp positions, paying funding

### Spot Account (Hyperliquid)

* **Collateral:** USDC and traded tokens
* **Shows:** Token balances
* **Used for:** Buying/selling HL-listed spot assets

### Spot Account (Solana)

* **Collateral:** SOL + any SPL token
* **Shows:** All token holdings
* **Used for:** Trading any Solana token via Jupiter Ultra

## Margin Allocation

Within your **Perps** account, you can further allocate margin:

* **Cross Margin**: Entire balance backs all positions
* **Isolated Margin**: Allocate specific margin per position

See [Margin & Leverage](/trading/margin-and-leverage.md) for details.

text:
# Overview

Prediction markets let you trade on the outcome of real-world events. Instead of speculating on asset prices, you buy shares representing your belief in whether something will or will not happen.

## How It Works

Every prediction market has two outcomes: **Yes** and **No**. Each outcome is represented by a token that trades between $0.00 and $1.00.

* A **Yes** share at $0.65 means the market believes there's a 65% chance the event happens
* A **No** share at $0.35 means the market believes there's a 35% chance it does not happen
* Yes + No prices always sum to $1.00

When the market resolves:

* Winning shares pay out $1.00 each
* Losing shares become worthless

Your profit is the difference between what you paid and the $1.00 payout.

## Example Trade

The market "Will BTC exceed $150k by March 2025?" shows:

* Yes: $0.42
* No: $0.58

You believe BTC will break $150k. You buy 100 Yes shares at $0.42, spending $42.

**If BTC exceeds $150k:** You redeem 100 shares at $1.00 each = $100. Profit: $58.

**If BTC does not exceed $150k:** Your shares are worth $0. Loss: $42.

## Market Types

Bullpen supports prediction markets across several categories:

| Category | Examples                                        |
| -------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Crypto   | Price movements, ETF approvals, protocol events |
| Politics | Elections, policy decisions, appointments       |
| Sports   | Game outcomes, championship winners             |
| Finance  | Earnings beats, Fed decisions, economic data    |
| Culture  | Awards, media events, celebrity outcomes        |

## Smart Money Tools

Bullpen surfaces smart money activity across all prediction markets. From the Predictions tab you can:

* View convergence signals when multiple smart wallets take the same side
* Track whale trades and biggest recent wins
* Filter live trade feeds by trader P\&L, volume, and trade size
* Copy trade profitable wallets with automated execution

Related pages:

* [Smart Money Overview](/smart-money/overview.md)
* [Copy Trading](/smart-money/copy-trading.md)

## Execution

All prediction market orders execute through Polymarket's Central Limit Order Book (CLOB). See [Polymarket Integration](/prediction-markets/polymarket-integration.md) for details on how orders are matched and settled.

## Key Differences from Perps

| Aspect      | Prediction Markets     | Perpetuals                    |
| ----------- | ---------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Underlying  | Event outcome (binary) | Asset price (continuous)      |
| Price range | $0.00 to $1.00         | Unlimited                     |
| Leverage    | None (1x only)         | Up to 50x                     |
| Settlement  | At market resolution   | Continuous (funding payments) |
| Expiry      | When event resolves    | Never                         |
| Collateral  | USDC.e on Polygon      | USDC on Hyperliquid           |

## Risk Considerations

* **Binary outcome**: You either win the full payout or lose your entire position
* **Resolution risk**: Markets can take time to resolve, locking your capital
* **Liquidity risk**: Some markets have thin orderbooks, leading to slippage
* **Oracle risk**: Resolution depends on accurate reporting of real-world events

## Getting Started

1. Fund your wallet with USDC.e on Polygon. See [Funding Your Wallet](/getting-started/funding-your-wallet.md).
2. Browse available markets
3. Place a limit or market order on your chosen outcome
4. Monitor your position until resolution
5. Redeem winning shares for $1.00 each

text:
# Polymarket Integration

## What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction market protocol built on Polygon. It operates a hybrid-decentralized Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) where order matching happens off-chain while settlement and execution occur on-chain.

The system has three core components:

* **CLOB Operator**: Handles order matching and book management off-chain
* **Exchange Contract**: Executes matched trades on-chain via atomic swaps
* **Conditional Token Framework (CTF)**: Gnosis-based ERC1155 tokens representing market outcomes

Bullpen routes prediction market orders to Polymarket's CLOB.

## How Orders Execute

1. You place an order through Bullpen
2. Order is signed with your wallet (EIP-712 structured data)
3. Submitted to Polymarket's CLOB operator
4. Operator matches your order against resting liquidity
5. Matched orders are submitted to the Exchange contract on Polygon
6. Trade settles on-chain, tokens transfer to your proxy wallet

Order matching is off-chain for speed. Settlement is on-chain for security and custody.

## Order Book Mechanics

Polymarket uses **price-time priority** with a unified orderbook:

1. Best prices execute first
2. At the same price, oldest orders fill first
3. Yes and No orders match against each other on a single book

The unified book means a Yes buy at $0.60 can match against a No buy at $0.40. When their prices sum to $1.00, the operator creates new outcome tokens from collateral and distributes them.

## Order Matching Types

Orders can match in three ways:

| Match Type | Description                                                       |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Fill**   | Your order matches against an existing resting order              |
| **Mint**   | Two opposing orders (Yes + No) summing to $1.00 create new tokens |
| **Merge**  | Two sell orders (Yes + No) burn tokens and release collateral     |

## Supported Order Types

Polymarket supports limit orders as the base primitive. Market orders are simulated by using marketable limit prices.

| Order Type | Behavior                                                        |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **GTC**    | Good-Til-Cancelled. Rests on the book until filled or cancelled |
| **FOK**    | Fill-Or-Kill. Must fill entirely and immediately, or cancel     |
| **GTD**    | Good-Til-Date. Expires at a specified timestamp                 |

See [Order Types](/prediction-markets/order-types.md) for details on each.

## Margin and Collateral

Prediction markets on Polymarket do not use leverage. All positions are fully collateralized.

When you place an order:

1. Your available USDC.e balance is checked
2. The order amount is reserved against your balance
3. You cannot place additional orders exceeding your remaining balance

Example: With 500 USDC.e, you can place one order to buy 1,000 Yes shares at $0.50. Any additional orders in that market will be rejected until the first order fills or is cancelled.

## Price Display

Prices shown on Polymarket represent the **midpoint** of the bid-ask spread, unless the spread exceeds $0.10. In wide-spread markets, the last traded price is displayed instead.

The orderbook displays:

* **Bid**: Highest price someone will pay
* **Ask**: Lowest price someone will sell
* **Spread**: Difference between bid and ask
* **Depth**: Total volume at each price level

## Fees

Polymarket charges a taker fee on certain markets. These fees fund Polymarket's [Maker Rebates Program](https://docs.polymarket.com/market-makers/maker-rebates), which redistributes fees daily to market makers to incentivize deeper liquidity and tighter spreads.

**Makers are never charged fees.** Only takers pay fees. **Geopolitical markets are fee-free.**

There are no Polymarket fees to deposit, withdraw, or redeem USDC

### Fee Formula

```
fee = C × feeRate × p × (1 - p)
```

Where **C** = number of shares traded and **p** = price of the shares. The effective rate peaks at 50% probability and decreases symmetrically toward $0.00 and $1.00.

#### Fee Structure by Category

| Category        | Taker Fee Rate | Maker Fee Rate | Maker Rebate |
| --------------- | -------------- | -------------- | ------------ |
| Crypto          | 0.072          | 0              | 20%          |
| Sports          | 0.03           | 0              | 25%          |
| Finance         | 0.04           | 0              | 25%          |
| Politics        | 0.04           | 0              | 25%          |
| Economics       | 0.05           | 0              | 25%          |
| Culture         | 0.05           | 0              | 25%          |
| Weather         | 0.05           | 0              | 25%          |
| Other / General | 0.05           | 0              | 25%          |
| Mentions        | 0.04           | 0              | 25%          |
| Tech            | 0.04           | 0              | 25%          |
| Geopolitics     | 0              | 0              | —            |

See [Polymarket's Fees Documentation](https://docs.polymarket.com/trading/fees) for the latest information.

## Conditional Tokens

Outcome shares are ERC-1155 tokens built on the Gnosis Conditional Token Framework (CTF). Each market has:

* A **condition ID** identifying the market
* Two **position IDs** for Yes and No outcomes
* **Collateral** backing the tokens (USDC.e)

Full details in [Positions and Settlement](/prediction-markets/positions-and-settlement.md).

## Multi-Outcome Markets

Some markets have more than two outcomes (e.g., "Who will win the election?" with multiple candidates).

These use the **NegRiskAdapter** contract:

* Each candidate gets their own Yes/No binary market
* Exactly one candidate's Yes resolves to $1.00
* All other candidates' Yes tokens resolve to $0.00
* No tokens can be converted across markets for capital efficiency

## Sports Markets

Sports markets have special rules:

* All outstanding limit orders are automatically cancelled when the game begins
* The orderbook is cleared at the official start time
* Only positions held at game start remain active

## Key Polymarket Resources

For deeper technical details:

* [Polymarket Docs: CLOB Introduction](https://docs.polymarket.com/developers/CLOB/introduction)
* [Order Types](https://docs.polymarket.com/developers/CLOB/orders/orders)
* [Conditional Token Framework](https://docs.polymarket.com/developers/CTF/merge)
* [Proxy Wallets](https://docs.polymarket.com/developers/proxy-wallet)
* [NegRisk Adapter](https://docs.polymarket.com/developers/neg-risk/overview)

text:
# Order Types

Polymarket's smart contracts only support limit orders as the underlying primitive. Market orders are simulated by submitting limit orders at prices guaranteed to cross the book.

## Limit Orders

A limit order specifies the maximum price you will pay (for buys) or minimum price you will accept (for sells). The order rests on the book until filled, cancelled, or expired.

### Parameters

* **Side**: Buy or Sell
* **Token**: Yes or No outcome token
* **Price**: Your limit price ($0.01 to $0.99)
* **Size**: Number of shares
* **Time-in-Force**: GTC, FOK, or GTD

### How It Works

1. You submit a limit buy for 100 Yes shares at $0.55
2. If resting asks exist at $0.55 or lower, your order fills immediately
3. Any unfilled portion rests on the book at $0.55
4. When a matching sell arrives, your order fills

### Partial Fills

Limit orders can partially fill. If you order 100 shares and only 40 are available at your price, you receive 40 shares and the remaining 60 rest on the book.

## Market Orders

Market orders execute immediately at the best available prices. Since Polymarket's contracts only accept limit orders, market orders are constructed by:

1. Scanning the orderbook for available liquidity
2. Calculating the worst price needed to fill your entire order
3. Submitting a limit order at that price

This guarantees immediate execution but not price. Large market orders can experience significant slippage.

### Use When

* You need immediate execution
* The orderbook has sufficient depth
* You accept the current market price

### Important Notes

* No price guarantee beyond the worst price in the book
* Check orderbook depth before placing large market orders
* Consider limit orders if you have time and want price control

## Time-in-Force Options

Time-in-force determines how long your order remains active.

### GTC (Good-Til-Cancelled)

The default option. Your order remains on the book until:

* Fully filled
* You cancel it
* The market resolves

Use GTC when you want to wait for your price.

### FOK (Fill-Or-Kill)

Your order must fill completely and immediately. If the full size cannot be matched against resting liquidity, the entire order is cancelled.

| Scenario                   | Result               |
| -------------------------- | -------------------- |
| Full size available        | Order fills entirely |
| Partial size available     | Order cancelled      |
| No liquidity at your price | Order cancelled      |

Use FOK for large orders where partial fills are unacceptable.

### GTD (Good-Til-Date)

Your order expires at a specified timestamp. Until then, it behaves like a GTC order.

Use GTD when you want exposure only until a certain time (e.g., before an event starts).

## Order Lifecycle

### Placement

1. You submit order parameters to Bullpen
2. Order is formatted as EIP-712 structured data
3. You sign the order with your wallet
4. Signed order is sent to Polymarket's CLOB operator

### Validation

The operator validates:

* Your signature is correct
* You have sufficient USDC.e balance or token balance
* Allowances are set for the Exchange contract
* Price and size are within valid ranges

Invalid orders are rejected immediately.

### Matching

Valid orders enter the matching engine:

1. **Marketable orders** cross against resting liquidity immediately
2. **Non-marketable orders** rest on the book awaiting a match
3. Matches are batched and submitted to the blockchain

### Settlement

Matched orders settle on Polygon:

1. The Exchange contract verifies both signatures
2. Tokens and collateral transfer atomically
3. Your proxy wallet receives the outcome tokens or USDC.e

### Monitoring

Orders are continuously monitored for validity:

* Balance checks ensure you can still cover the order
* Allowance checks verify contract permissions
* On-chain cancellations are detected

Orders that become invalid are removed from the book.

## Order Constraints

### Balance Reservation

Your USDC.e balance is reserved when you place buy orders. You cannot place orders exceeding your available balance for each market.

Example:

* Balance: 500 USDC.e
* Order 1: Buy 1,000 Yes at $0.50 (reserves 500 USDC.e)
* Order 2: Any additional buy order in this market is rejected

### Price Bounds

* Minimum price: $0.01
* Maximum price: $0.99
* Prices outside this range are rejected

### Size Limits

Minimum and maximum order sizes may apply depending on the market. Check individual market parameters.

## Best Practices

* **Use limit orders** when you have a target price and can wait
* **Check depth** before market orders to estimate slippage
* **Use FOK** for large orders that must fill completely
* **Cancel stale orders** to free up reserved balance
* **Monitor fills** since partial fills are common with limit orders

text:
# Positions and Settlement

## Outcome Tokens

Every prediction market has two outcome tokens: **Yes** and **No**. These are ERC-1155 tokens built on the Gnosis Conditional Token Framework (CTF).

Each token represents a claim on $1.00 if its outcome is correct when the market resolves.

| Token | Pays $1.00 if...      | Worth $0.00 if...     |
| ----- | --------------------- | --------------------- |
| Yes   | Event happens         | Event does not happen |
| No    | Event does not happen | Event happens         |

## Token Economics

The Yes and No token prices always sum to approximately $1.00. This is enforced by arbitrage:

* If Yes + No > $1.00, arbitrageurs sell both and profit
* If Yes + No < $1.00, arbitrageurs buy both and redeem for $1.00

This relationship means:

```
Yes Price + No Price = $1.00
Yes Price = 1 - No Price
```

Buying Yes at $0.60 is economically equivalent to selling No at $0.40.

## Splits (Minting New Tokens)

A **split** creates new outcome tokens from collateral. When two orders match and their prices sum to $1.00:

1. The buyer pays $0.60 for Yes
2. Another buyer pays $0.40 for No
3. Their combined $1.00 is deposited as collateral
4. The CTF contract mints 1 Yes token and 1 No token
5. Each buyer receives their respective token

This is how new shares enter the market. Every Yes/No pair in existence is backed by exactly $1.00 of collateral.

### Split Example

* Trader A: Buy 100 Yes at $0.60 ($60 total)
* Trader B: Buy 100 No at $0.40 ($40 total)
* Combined: $100 collateral deposited
* Result: 100 Yes tokens to A, 100 No tokens to B

## Merges (Burning Tokens)

A **merge** destroys outcome tokens and releases collateral. When you hold equal amounts of Yes and No tokens:

1. You submit both tokens to the CTF contract
2. The contract burns the token pair
3. You receive $1.00 per pair (minus any fees)

Merges can also happen during matching:

* Trader A: Sell 100 Yes at $0.60
* Trader B: Sell 100 No at $0.40
* Combined: 100 Yes + 100 No tokens burned
* Result: $60 to A, $40 to B

### Merge Example

You hold 50 Yes tokens and 50 No tokens in the same market. You can merge them:

* Input: 50 Yes + 50 No
* Output: $50.00 USDC.e (minus fees)

This is useful for exiting a hedged position or recovering capital without waiting for resolution.

## Converting Positions

### Buying Yes vs. Selling No

These are different actions with similar economic outcomes:

| Action  | What Happens                      | Net Effect        |
| ------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Buy Yes | Pay USDC.e, receive Yes tokens    | Long Yes exposure |
| Sell No | Give up No tokens, receive USDC.e | Long Yes exposure |

Buying Yes requires USDC.e. Selling No requires owning No tokens.

### Position Conversion

You can convert between positions using splits and merges:

**Convert No to Yes:**

1. Buy additional No tokens to pair with your Yes position
2. Merge them to release collateral
3. Use collateral to buy Yes

**Convert Yes to No:**

1. Buy additional Yes tokens to pair with your No position
2. Merge them to release collateral
3. Use collateral to buy No

## Multi-Outcome Markets

Markets with more than two outcomes (e.g., "Who will win?") use the **NegRiskAdapter**:

* Each candidate is a separate binary Yes/No market
* All markets share a common collateral pool via WrappedCollateral
* Exactly one candidate's Yes token will resolve to $1.00
* All other Yes tokens resolve to $0.00

### Conversion in Multi-Outcome Markets

The NegRiskAdapter enables capital-efficient conversions:

* A No position in one market can convert to Yes positions in all other markets
* This is because betting against one candidate is equivalent to betting for all others

Example with 3 candidates (A, B, C):

* Holding No on Candidate A means you believe B or C will win
* This can convert to Yes positions on both B and C

## Market Resolution

When an event's outcome is determined, the market resolves:

### Resolution Process

1. **Proposal**: Someone proposes the outcome by staking a $750 USDC.e bond
2. **Challenge Period**: 2 hours for disputes
3. **Finalization**: If unchallenged, the outcome is finalized
4. **Redemption**: Winners can claim $1.00 per share

### Disputes

If someone disputes the proposed outcome:

1. The disputer stakes their own bond
2. The dispute goes to UMA's Data Verification Mechanism (DVM)
3. UMA token holders vote on the correct outcome
4. Winner receives the loser's bond as reward

Disputed resolutions can take 4-5 days or longer.

### Oracle System

Polymarket uses UMA Protocol as its oracle:

* **Optimistic Oracle**: Assumes proposals are correct unless disputed
* **Decentralized Voting**: UMA stakers resolve disputes
* **Economic Security**: Bond requirements deter false proposals

## Claiming Winnings

After a market resolves:

### Redemption Steps

1. Visit the market page on Polymarket or Bullpen
2. Click "Redeem" on your winning position
3. Sign the transaction
4. Receive $1.00 per winning share in USDC.e

### Important Notes

* Winning shares: Redeem for $1.00 each
* Losing shares: Worth $0.00, can be cleared from portfolio
* No fee for redemption
* Capital is locked during the resolution period

### Clearing Losing Positions

Losing shares do not automatically disappear. Visit the market page to clear them from your portfolio. This is a housekeeping action with no financial impact.

## Settlement Timing

| Scenario          | Typical Duration   |
| ----------------- | ------------------ |
| Undisputed market | 2+ hours           |
| Disputed market   | 4-5 days or longer |
| Sports markets    | Usually same day   |

Your capital remains locked until resolution completes. Factor this into your trading decisions.

# Wallets and Collateral

## Collateral: USDC.e

All prediction markets on Polymarket are denominated and settled in **USDC.e** on the Polygon network.

### What is USDC.e?

USDC.e is a bridged version of USDC from Ethereum to Polygon. It maintains 1:1 backing with USDC on Ethereum while enabling faster, cheaper transactions on Polygon.

| Property | USDC.e                                       |
| -------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| Network  | Polygon PoS                                  |
| Contract | `0x2791bca1f2de4661ed88a30c99a7a9449aa84174` |
| Decimals | 6                                            |
| Backing  | 1:1 with USDC on Ethereum                    |

### USDC.e vs Native USDC

Polygon has two versions of USDC:

| Token      | Description                                   |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| **USDC.e** | Bridged from Ethereum, used by Polymarket     |
| **USDC**   | Native Polygon USDC issued directly by Circle |

Polymarket uses USDC.e. Depositing native USDC requires bridging or swapping first.

## Depositing Funds

### From Ethereum

1. Send USDC to your Polymarket Ethereum deposit address
2. Polymarket bridges it to Polygon as USDC.e
3. Funds arrive in \~30 minutes
4. Gas fees apply (\~$30+ depending on network conditions)

### From Polygon

1. Send USDC.e directly to your Polygon deposit address
2. Funds arrive in a few minutes
3. Gas fees are minimal (a few cents)

Sending USDC.e over Polygon is faster and cheaper than bridging from Ethereum.

## Proxy Wallet System

When you first trade on Polymarket, a **proxy wallet** is deployed for you on Polygon. This is where your positions and USDC.e are held.

### How It Works

1. You connect with your EOA (MetaMask, hardware wallet, etc.) or Magic.link email wallet
2. A 1-of-1 multisig Safe is deployed on Polygon
3. Your connecting wallet is the sole owner/signer
4. All trading occurs through this proxy wallet

### Architecture

```
Your Wallet (EOA or Magic)
         │
         │ owns
         ▼
   Proxy Wallet (Safe)
         │
         │ holds
         ▼
  USDC.e + Outcome Tokens
```

The proxy wallet is a Gnosis Safe with a single signer (you). It provides several benefits:

* **Gasless trading**: Polymarket relays transactions and pays gas
* **Atomic execution**: Multi-step operations happen in one transaction
* **No approval popups**: Allowances are pre-set for the Exchange contract

### Signature Types

Polymarket supports multiple wallet configurations:

| Signature Type | Description                      | Use Case                   |
| -------------- | -------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| **Type 0**     | Standard EOA signature (EIP-712) | MetaMask, hardware wallets |
| **Type 1**     | Magic.link email wallet          | Email-based login          |
| **Type 2**     | Browser wallet proxy (Safe)      | Most web users             |

The signature type tells Polymarket how to verify your order signatures.

## Wallet Security

### Custody Model

Polymarket uses a non-custodial model:

* You control the signing key
* Only you can authorize transactions
* Polymarket can relay transactions on your behalf (after you sign)
* Polymarket cannot move funds without your signature

### The Safe Contract

Your proxy wallet is a 1-of-1 Gnosis Safe:

* Battle-tested smart contract wallet
* Single owner (your connected wallet)
* Audited by multiple security firms
* Used across DeFi for billions in assets

### Private Key Access

You retain full control:

* Your EOA private key controls the proxy wallet
* Export your key from MetaMask or Magic.link
* Transfer assets using your EOA at any time

## Importing and Exporting Wallets

### Exporting from Magic.link

If you signed up with email (Magic.link):

1. Log in to your Polymarket account
2. Go to Cash > three dots menu > Export Private Key
3. Or visit: `https://reveal.magic.link/polymarket` while logged in
4. Copy your private key securely

### Importing to MetaMask

1. Open MetaMask
2. Click your account icon > Import Account
3. Paste your exported private key
4. Your EOA is now accessible in MetaMask

### EOA vs Magic Wallet Limitations

| Feature                 | EOA (MetaMask)  | Magic.link            |
| ----------------------- | --------------- | --------------------- |
| Private key export      | Yes             | Yes                   |
| Import to other wallets | Yes             | Yes                   |
| Programmatic trading    | Straightforward | Requires funder setup |
| Hardware wallet support | Yes             | No                    |

**Important**: Only EOAs can be directly imported to external wallets or trading bots. Magic.link wallets can export their private key and then be imported elsewhere, but the proxy wallet (Safe) itself cannot be imported directly.

### Programmatic Access

For API or bot trading:

**EOA Users:**

1. Use your private key directly
2. Set `signature_type=0`
3. Set allowances for the Exchange contract

**Magic.link Users:**

1. Export your private key
2. Use `signature_type=1` with the funder address parameter
3. The funder address is your proxy wallet address

## Allowances

Before trading programmatically, you must set token allowances:

### Required Allowances

| Token      | Contract          | Purpose                |
| ---------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| USDC.e     | Exchange contract | Buying outcome tokens  |
| CTF tokens | Exchange contract | Selling outcome tokens |

### Setting Allowances

For web users, Polymarket handles this automatically through the proxy wallet.

For programmatic users:

1. Approve the Exchange contract to spend your USDC.e
2. Approve the Exchange contract to transfer your CTF tokens
3. Allowances must be set from the funder address

## Balance Management

### Checking Balances

Your trading balance is:

```
Available = USDC.e Balance - Reserved for Open Orders
```

### Reserved Funds

When you place buy orders, the USDC.e is reserved:

* A $50 order at $0.50 reserves $25
* This amount is unavailable for other orders
* Cancelling the order releases the reservation

### Insufficient Balance Errors

If you see "not enough balance/allowance":

1. Check your USDC.e balance in the proxy wallet
2. Cancel open orders to free reserved funds
3. Verify allowances are set (for programmatic trading)
4. Ensure you have enough for the order size at your price

# Copy Trading

When you copy a wallet, Bullpen mirrors that wallet's buys and sells in your account. You control allocation, filters, and how exits are handled.

## How to Start Copying

1. Find a wallet through Discover, Trending, Walletscope, or the tracker
2. Click **Copy Trade**
3. Configure your settings
4. Click **Start Copying**

You can also go to **Copying** and click **+ Copy a Trader**, then paste a wallet address directly.

## Copy Trade Settings

When you set up a copy trade, you see the wallet's stats, including lifetime PNL, biggest win, and total predictions.

### Allocation

| Setting                          | Options               |
| -------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| Amount per copy trade            | Fixed or % of Balance |
| Max allocated to a single market | custom                |
| Minimum per trade                | $5                    |

### Sell Behavior

When the copied wallet sells, choose how Bullpen handles it:

| Option            | What Happens                                             |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Auto-sell**     | Bullpen automatically sells your position when they sell |
| **Alert me**      | You get a notification and decide manually               |
| **Manage myself** | No action taken on sells                                 |

### Advanced Settings

| Setting                | Description                                                              |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Total Budget           | Max capital across all copy trades for this wallet. No limit by default. |
| Price Range            | Only copy trades within a share price range. Default is 5¢ to 95¢.       |
| Min Time to Resolution | Skip markets resolving within 24h, 7d, or 30d.                           |
| Copy Trade Size        | Minimum and maximum trade size filters.                                  |
| Slippage Tolerance     | 1%, 2%, 3%, 5%, or a custom value.                                       |
| Execution Mode         | **Auto** executes immediately. **Confirm** waits for your approval.      |
| Nickname               | Optional label for the copied wallet.                                    |

## Managing Active Copies

Go to **Copying** to see all active copies.

The dashboard shows:

| Column         | Description                         |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| Wallet         | Trader name and address             |
| Status         | Active or Paused                    |
| Spent / Budget | Capital used vs. budget limit       |
| Sold           | Number of positions sold            |
| Buy / Sell     | Trade counts                        |
| Your PNL       | Your profit/loss from copied trades |
| Actions        | Edit settings, pause, or delete     |

The right panel shows three tabs:

* **Copied Trades**: Trades that were successfully copied to your account
* **All Trades**: Every trade the wallet made, including ones you did not copy
* **Skipped**: Trades that did not match your filters

Click the edit icon on any wallet to adjust settings or click **Stop Copying** to end.

## Tracking Without Copying

If you want to follow a wallet without mirroring trades, use **Track** instead of **Copy Trade**. Tracked wallets appear in **All Wallets**.

The **Tracker Feed** on the right panel shows real-time trades from all tracked wallets. From the All Wallets view you can:

| Action | Description                                |
| ------ | ------------------------------------------ |
| Edit   | Update wallet nickname or settings         |
| Copy   | Switch from tracking to copy trading       |
| Alert  | Set up trade notifications for this wallet |
| Delete | Remove from tracker                        |

## Sharing & Importing Groups

Share your wallet groups with other Bullpen users. When you share a group, others can import your entire list of tracked or copied wallets.

### Share a Group

1. Hover over the group
2. Click the share icon next to any group
3. Copy the code or the link

The share link includes your **referral tag**. Anyone who imports your group through the link counts as your referral.

### Import a Group

1. Go to **Add Group**
2. Click **Import a group**
3. Enter the code or paste the link

The imported wallets appear as a new group in your sidebar. From there you can track them, copy trade them, or set up alerts.

# Smart Money Discovery

Bullpen surfaces smart money activity through three views in the Predictions tab: Discover, Walletscope, and Eventscope. Each provides a different angle on what smart traders are doing.

## Discover

The Discover view is split into three columns:

**Smart Money:** Shows when multiple smart traders are buying the same outcome on a market. Displays the number of wallets, total dollar amount, win rate, and lifetime P\&L. Click **Trade** to take the same position.

**Whales:** Individual large trades from wallets with strong track records. Each entry shows the trader's win rate, lifetime P\&L, trade size, and market. Click **Copy Trade** to mirror their trade or **Track** to follow without copying.

**Biggest Wins:** Recent high-P\&L trades sorted by amount won.

## Walletscope

Real-time feed of trades from wallets across Polymarket. Use the filter panel on the left to narrow results by trader quality:

| Filter                       | Description                        |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Min. Lifetime PNL            | Minimum lifetime profit            |
| Min. Lifetime Volume         | Minimum total volume traded        |
| Min. Lifetime Predictions    | Minimum number of predictions      |
| Min. Current Positions Value | Minimum active position value      |
| Min. Position Size           | Minimum individual position size   |
| Min. Trade Size              | Minimum trade amount               |
| Share Price Range            | Filter by min/max share price      |
| Category                     | Filter by market category          |
| Action                       | All, Buy, or Sell                  |
| Show Tracking Only           | Limit to wallets you already track |

Toggle between **Trades** and **Comments** at the top of the filter panel to switch between trade activity and trader commentary.

Click **Create Trade Alert** at the bottom of the filter panel to get notified when matching trades come through.

## Eventscope

Live trade feed showing every trade across Polymarket events. Each row shows:

| Column    | Description                            |
| --------- | -------------------------------------- |
| Type      | Buy or Sell                            |
| Amount    | Dollar amount and number of shares     |
| Trader    | Wallet name or address                 |
| Outcome   | The outcome and current price          |
| Market    | The prediction market                  |
| Timestamp | When the trade happened                |
| Quick Buy | One-click button to take the same side |

Filter by category, action, minimum trade size, and share price range using the controls at the top.

Click **Create Alert from Filters** to set up notifications for trades matching your criteria.

# Convergence Signals

The **Trending** view in the Predictions tab shows markets with active smart money flow. When multiple profitable wallets independently take the same side of a market, that is a convergence signal.

## Signal Strength

Markets are grouped by how many smart wallets are active:

| Signal         | Description                                              |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Heating Up** | 2-4 smart wallets active on the same market              |
| **Trending**   | Higher smart wallet concentration                        |
| **New**        | Recently created markets with early smart money activity |

Each market card shows the event, Yes/No outcomes with current prices, and at the bottom: which whales are in, the total dollar amount, and which side they're buying.

Example: "$2.2M of Yes bought by 4 whales" means four independently profitable wallets have all taken the Yes side.

## Filters

| Filter   | Options                                                     |
| -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| Show     | All, Trending, Heating Up, New                              |
| Time     | 24h, 7d, 30d                                                |
| Category | All, Crypto, Politics, Sports                               |
| Hide     | Crypto, Sports (toggle specific categories off)             |
| Max Odds | Filter out markets above a certain probability (e.g., <95%) |

## Trading from Convergence

Each card has **Buy Yes** and **Buy No** buttons. Click to place a trade directly from the Trending view.

Expand the whale details at the bottom of each card to see which specific wallets are involved, their trade sizes, and their profiles.

# Smart Money Alerts

You can create smart money alerts from two places in the Predictions tab:

| Source          | How                                                   |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| **Walletscope** | Set your filters, click **Create Trade Alert**        |
| **Eventscope**  | Set your filters, click **Create Alert from Filters** |

## Walletscope Alerts

Set filters for the type of trader activity you want to monitor:

| Filter                       | Description                                          |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| Min. Lifetime PNL            | Only traders above a P\&L threshold                  |
| Min. Lifetime Volume         | Only traders above a volume threshold                |
| Min. Lifetime Predictions    | Only traders with enough history                     |

| Min. Trade Size              | Minimum trade amount                                 |

You can also set alerts on individual wallets from the tracker. Open **All Wallets**, click the alert icon on any wallet, and configure:

| Action             | All, Buy, or Sell              |

## Managing Alerts

Your alerts live in the **Alerts** tab in the bottom navigation bar. From there you can edit, pause, or delete them.

# Overview

## Market Types

| Market                 | Description                               | Execution          |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| **Prediction Markets** | Event-driven markets with binary outcomes | Polymarket         |
| **Perpetuals**         | Leveraged derivatives with no expiry      | Hyperliquid        |
| **Spot (Major)**       | Large-cap tokens listed on Hyperliquid    | UNIT / Hyperliquid |
| **Spot (Long-tail)**   | Any Solana token (SPL and Token2022)      | Jupiter Ultra      |

Support for additional chains is planned for future releases.

Each market type has its own account balance. You can transfer collateral between accounts using the transfer module in the app.

## Prediction Markets

Prediction markets let you trade on the outcome of real-world events. Bullpen routes these orders through Polymarket's CLOB.

Key characteristics:

* Binary outcomes priced between $0.00 and $1.00
* Fully collateralized with USDC.e on Polygon
* Smart money discovery, copy trading, and alerts built in

→ [Learn more about Prediction Markets](/prediction-markets/overview.md)

## Perpetuals

Perpetual futures let you take leveraged long or short positions on crypto assets. Positions have no expiration date and remain open until you close them or get liquidated.

Key characteristics:

* USDC-margined
* Up to 50x leverage (varies by asset)
* Funding rate payments every hour
* Cross or isolated margin modes

→ [Learn more about Perpetuals](/trading/perpetuals.md)

## Spot Trading

Spot trading means buying and selling the actual underlying asset.

**Major assets** (BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, etc.) trade on Hyperliquid's spot orderbook via the UNIT integration. These offer deep liquidity and tight spreads.

**Long-tail assets** (any Solana token) route through Jupiter Ultra, which aggregates liquidity across all major Solana DEXs and provides MEV protection.

→ [Learn more about Spot Trading](/trading/spot-trading.md)

## Order Types

Bullpen supports the full range of Hyperliquid order types for perps:

* Market
* Limit (GTC, IOC, ALO)
* Stop Market / Stop Limit
* Take Profit / Stop Loss
* TWAP
* Scale Orders

→ [Full Order Types Reference](/trading/order-types.md)

text:
# Perpetuals

## How Perps Work

A perpetual future lets you speculate on price movements with leverage. Unlike traditional futures, perps never expire. You can hold a position indefinitely as long as you maintain sufficient margin.

**Long position**: You profit when the price goes up.\
**Short position**: You profit when the price goes down.

## Key Concepts

### Funding Rate

Funding keeps the perp price aligned with the spot (index) price. Every hour, traders exchange payments:

* If perp price > index price → longs pay shorts
* If perp price < index price → shorts pay longs

The funding rate displayed in the interface shows the expected payment as a percentage. Positive means longs pay; negative means shorts pay.

### Oracle Price

The oracle price is the reference price derived from external spot markets. It's used to calculate funding rates and determine liquidation thresholds.

For detailed oracle methodology, see [Hyperliquid Docs: Oracle](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/hypercore/oracle).

### Mark Price

The mark price is used for:

* Calculating unrealized PnL
* Margin requirements
* Triggering TP/SL orders
* Liquidations

It's designed to be manipulation-resistant by incorporating multiple price inputs.

For details, see [Hyperliquid Docs: Robust Price Indices](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/robust-price-indices).

### Margin

Bullpen uses USDC as collateral for perps. You can choose between:

**Cross Margin**: Your entire perps balance serves as collateral across all positions. Gains from one position can offset losses from another.

**Isolated Margin**: Collateral is allocated to each position individually. A liquidation in one position won't affect others.

## Leverage

Maximum leverage varies by asset. Major pairs like BTC and ETH support up to 50x. Smaller assets may have lower limits.

Higher leverage = higher risk. A 50x position gets liquidated with just a 2% adverse move.

## Liquidations

If your position's margin ratio falls below the maintenance requirement, you get liquidated. The liquidation engine closes your position at the current mark price.

To avoid liquidation:

* Use lower leverage
* Set stop losses
* Monitor your margin ratio
* Add margin to at-risk positions

For liquidation mechanics, see [Hyperliquid Docs: Liquidations](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/liquidations).

## Available Markets

Bullpen supports all perpetual markets listed on Hyperliquid. This includes major assets (BTC, ETH, SOL) as well as a wide range of altcoins and memecoins.

View the full list in the app or check [Hyperliquid's perpetual assets](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/perpetual-assets).

text:
# Spot Trading

## Routing Logic

| Asset Type              | Route              | Examples                 |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------ |
| Hyperliquid-listed spot | UNIT → Hyperliquid | BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, MON |
| Solana SPL tokens       | Jupiter Ultra      | Any token on Solana      |

## Hyperliquid Spot (via UNIT)

Major assets listed on Hyperliquid's spot market execute through the UNIT integration. These include:

* HYPE/USDC
* BTC/USDC
* ETH/USDC
* SOL/USDC
* MON/USDC
* XPL/USDC
* USDT/USDC
* FARTCOIN/USDC
* PUMP/USDC

Benefits of Hyperliquid spot:

* Onchain orderbook with deep liquidity
* Tight spreads on major pairs
* Same execution quality as perps
* USDC settlement

The full list of supported assets appears in the Markets tab within the app.

## Solana Spot (via Jupiter Ultra)

For any Solana token not listed on Hyperliquid, trades route through Jupiter Ultra.

### What is Jupiter Ultra?

Jupiter Ultra is a meta-aggregation layer that finds the best execution across all Solana liquidity sources. It goes beyond simple DEX aggregation by:

**Comparing actual execution vs. quoted prices**: Ultra simulates trades before routing to predict real slippage, not just quoted prices.

**MEV Protection**: Transactions execute via ShadowLane, Jupiter's proprietary landing engine. Trades never touch external mempools, eliminating sandwich attack risk.

**Sub-second landing**: 50-400ms transaction times vs. 400ms-1.2s for traditional methods.

### Liquidity Sources

Ultra aggregates from:

* Iris (Jupiter's own router)
* JupiterZ (zero-slippage engine)
* DFlow
* Hashflow
* OKX
* All major Solana AMMs

### Gasless Swaps

For trades above \~$10 USD, Ultra can cover gas fees automatically. The fee is deducted from your swap amount, so no SOL balance is required.

### Trade Settings

You can customize execution in Settings:

| Setting            | Description                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Speed Mode**     | Fast (higher slippage tolerance) or Economy (lower fees) |
| **Priority Fee**   | Extra fee to validators for faster confirmation          |
| **Slippage**       | Maximum acceptable price movement                        |
| **MEV Protection** | On (better pricing, safer) or Off (faster)               |

For most trades, the defaults work well. Adjust for volatile tokens or time-sensitive trades.

## Finding Tokens

**Search**: Enter contract address, ticker, or token name in the search bar.

**Trending**: Browse featured and trending tokens on the Discover page.

**Telegram Bot**: Paste any token address in a Bullpen-enabled chat. The bot analyzes the token and lets you trade directly.

**Runners & Memescope**: Use the discovery tools to find new tokens before they trend. See [Runners & Memescope](/features/runners-memescope.md).

text:
# Order Types

## Quick Reference

| Order Type                                           | Best For                | Execution                    | Markets                  |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| [Market](/trading/order-types/market-order.md)       | Immediate entry/exit    | Instant, any price           | Perps, Spot, Predictions |
| [Limit](/trading/order-types/limit-order.md)         | Price control           | At your price or better      | Perps, Spot, Predictions |
| [Stop Market](/trading/order-types/stop-market.md)   | Stop losses, breakouts  | Market order when triggered  | Perps, Spot              |
| [Stop Limit](/trading/order-types/stop-limit.md)     | Controlled stops        | Limit order when triggered   | Perps, Spot              |
| [TP/SL](/trading/order-types/tp-sl.md)               | Risk management         | Auto profit/loss exits       | Perps, Spot              |
| [TWAP](/trading/order-types/twap.md)                 | Large orders            | Time-distributed execution   | Perps, Spot              |
| [Scale Orders](/trading/order-types/scale-orders.md) | DCA, gradual entry/exit | Multiple limits across range | Perps, Spot              |

## Order Type Categories

### Basic Orders (All Markets)

* [**Market Order**](/trading/order-types/market-order.md): Execute immediately at best available price
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot, Prediction Markets
* [**Limit Order**](/trading/order-types/limit-order.md): Execute at your specified price or better
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot, Prediction Markets

### Conditional Orders (Perps & Spot Only)

* [**Stop Market**](/trading/order-types/stop-market.md): Trigger market order at price level
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot
* [**Stop Limit**](/trading/order-types/stop-limit.md): Trigger limit order at price level
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot
* [**Take Profit / Stop Loss**](/trading/order-types/tp-sl.md): Automated profit taking and loss limits
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot

### Advanced Orders (Perps & Spot Only)

* [**TWAP**](/trading/order-types/twap.md): Time-weighted execution for large orders
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot
* [**Scale Orders**](/trading/order-types/scale-orders.md): Distribute orders across price range
  * *Available for*: Perpetuals, Spot

## Important Concepts

### [Time-in-Force (TIF) Options](/trading/order-types/time-in-force.md)

Controls how long your orders stay active:

* **GTC** (Good 'Til Canceled): Stays active until filled or canceled
* **GTD** (Good 'Til Date): Active until specific date/time
* **IOC** (Immediate or Cancel): Fill immediately or cancel
* **FOK** (Fill or Kill): Fill completely or cancel
* **ALO** (Add Liquidity Only): Only add to orderbook (maker only)

[Learn more about Time-in-Force →](/trading/order-types/time-in-force.md)

### [Reduce Only](/trading/order-types/reduce-only.md)

Optional flag that ensures orders can only close positions, not open new ones.

[Learn more about Reduce Only →](/trading/order-types/reduce-only.md)

## Order Lifecycle

1. **Placed**: Order submitted to Hyperliquid
2. **Resting**: Limit order sitting on the order book
3. **Triggered**: Stop/TP/SL condition met, order sent to book
4. **Filled**: Order executed (partially or fully)
5. **Canceled**: Order removed (by you or system)

View all order states in the Order History tab.

## Choosing the Right Order Type

### For Immediate Execution

Use [**Market Order**](/trading/order-types/market-order.md) when speed matters more than price.

### For Price Control

Use [**Limit Order**](/trading/order-types/limit-order.md) when you want a specific entry/exit price.

### For Risk Management

Use [**TP/SL Orders**](/trading/order-types/tp-sl.md) on every position to automate exits.

### For Large Positions

Use [**TWAP**](/trading/order-types/twap.md) to minimize market impact and slippage.

### For Gradual Entry/Exit

Use [**Scale Orders**](/trading/order-types/scale-orders.md) to DCA or distribute across price levels.

### For Breakouts and Stops

Use [**Stop Market**](/trading/order-types/stop-market.md) for guaranteed execution after trigger. Use [**Stop Limit**](/trading/order-types/stop-limit.md) for price control after trigger.

## Next Steps

* [Perpetuals Trading Guide](/trading/perpetuals.md)
* [Spot Trading Guide](/trading/spot-trading.md)
* [Margin & Leverage](/trading/margin-and-leverage.md)

text:
# Margin & Leverage

## Collateral

Bullpen uses **USDC** as collateral for perpetual futures on Hyperliquid. Your perps account balance determines how much margin you have available.

## Margin Modes

### Cross Margin

Your entire account balance backs all positions. Unrealized gains from one position can offset unrealized losses from another.

**Pros**:

* More efficient capital use
* Lower liquidation risk for diversified positions
* Profits automatically available as margin

**Cons**:

* One bad position can liquidate your entire account
* Harder to manage risk per trade

### Isolated Margin

Each position has its own dedicated margin. Losses are limited to the margin allocated to that specific position.

**Pros**:

* Risk contained per position
* Liquidation in one position doesn't affect others
* Clearer risk management

**Cons**:

* Less capital efficient
* Must manually allocate margin to each position

## Leverage

Leverage multiplies your exposure relative to your margin.

| Leverage | Margin Required | Liquidation Move |
| -------- | --------------- | ---------------- |
| 1x       | 100%            | \~100%           |
| 5x       | 20%             | \~20%            |
| 10x      | 10%             | \~10%            |
| 25x      | 4%              | \~4%             |
| 50x      | 2%              | \~2%             |

*Liquidation thresholds are approximate. Actual values depend on maintenance margin requirements.*

### Maximum Leverage

Max leverage varies by asset:

* **BTC, ETH**: Up to 50x
* **Major alts**: Typically 20-50x
* **Smaller assets**: Often 5-20x

Check the trading interface for the specific max leverage on each asset.

### Adjusting Leverage

You can change leverage for a position at any time:

1. Open the position details
2. Click the leverage selector
3. Choose new leverage level

Increasing leverage reduces your margin buffer. Decreasing leverage requires additional margin (or closes part of your position if insufficient).

## Margin Requirements

### Initial Margin

The minimum margin required to open a position.

```
Initial Margin = Position Size / Leverage
```

### Maintenance Margin

The minimum margin required to keep a position open. If your margin falls below this level, you get liquidated.

Maintenance margin is typically lower than initial margin, giving you a buffer.

### Margin Ratio

```
Margin Ratio = Account Equity / Position Value
```

When your margin ratio falls below the maintenance requirement, liquidation begins.

## Adding Margin

To reduce liquidation risk on an existing position:

1. Transfer more USDC to your perps account
2. Close other positions to free up margin
3. Reduce position size

In isolated margin mode, you can also add margin directly to a specific position.

## Liquidations

When margin ratio drops below maintenance:

1. Liquidation engine takes over
2. Position is closed at mark price
3. Remaining margin (if any) returns to your account
4. If underwater, losses are socialized via ADL

For liquidation mechanics, see [Hyperliquid Docs: Liquidations](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/liquidations).

## Risk Management Tips

* Start with lower leverage until you understand the mechanics
* Always use stop losses
* Don't use your entire balance as margin
* Monitor positions during high volatility
* Understand funding rate exposure on longer holds

# Trade Routing

## Routing Decision Tree

```
Is it a perpetual futures trade?
├── Yes → Hyperliquid
└── No (Spot trade)
    ├── Is the asset listed on Hyperliquid spot?
    │   ├── Yes → UNIT (Hyperliquid spot orderbook)
    │   └── No → Jupiter Ultra (Solana)
```

You don't need to choose venues manually. Bullpen handles routing behind the scenes.

## Why This Architecture?

Each venue is optimized for different use cases:

| Venue             | Best For          | Why                                         |
| ----------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| **Hyperliquid**   | Perps, major spot | Onchain CLOB, deep liquidity, low latency   |
| **UNIT**          | HL-listed spot    | Direct access to Hyperliquid spot book      |
| **Jupiter Ultra** | Solana tokens     | Meta-aggregation, MEV protection, any token |

## Execution Quality

### Hyperliquid (Perps & Spot)

Hyperliquid operates a fully onchain central limit order book. Key properties:

* **Deterministic execution**: Every order, cancel, and trade recorded onchain
* **Price-time priority**: Best prices first, oldest orders first at same price
* **Block-level finality**: Trades settle in the same block
* **No front-running**: Matching happens in consensus layer

Latency is sub-second for order placement and matching.

### Jupiter Ultra (Solana Spot)

Ultra provides institutional-grade execution for Solana tokens:

* **Meta-aggregation**: Compares routes across Iris, JupiterZ, DFlow, Hashflow, OKX
* **Predictive execution**: Simulates actual execution quality, not just quoted prices
* **MEV protection**: ShadowLane keeps transactions private until landing
* **Sub-second landing**: 50-400ms typical

On average, Jupiter Ultra achieves **+0.63 bps positive slippage**, meaning you get slightly more than quoted.

## Slippage & Price Impact

### Perps

Slippage depends on:

* Order size relative to book depth
* Current spread
* Market volatility

Use limit orders to control execution price. TWAP for large orders.

### Spot (Hyperliquid)

Same mechanics as perps. Orderbook-based execution.

### Spot (Solana via Ultra)

Ultra's Real-Time Slippage Estimator (RTSE) dynamically sets appropriate slippage based on:

* Token volatility history
* Current liquidity depth
* Recent execution patterns

You can override with manual slippage in Settings.

## Fees

| Venue             | Maker  | Taker           |
| ----------------- | ------ | --------------- |
| Hyperliquid Perps | Rebate | 0.025%          |
| Hyperliquid Spot  | Varies | Varies          |
| Jupiter Ultra     | N/A    | Route-dependent |

*Fees subject to change. Check current rates in the app.*

Builder codes and referral discounts may apply. See Hyperliquid docs for current fee schedules.

## Failed Transactions

### Hyperliquid

Orders can fail if:

* Insufficient margin
* Price moved past limit
* Position would exceed max size

Failed orders return an error immediately. No funds are lost.

### Solana (Jupiter)

Transactions can fail due to:

* Slippage exceeded
* Insufficient SOL for gas (if gasless not available)
* Network congestion

Jupiter Ultra's high landing rate (sub-second) minimizes failures. If a swap fails, your tokens remain in your wallet.

text:
# Hyperliquid Integration

## What is Hyperliquid?

Hyperliquid is a Layer 1 blockchain purpose-built for trading. It runs a fully onchain central limit order book (CLOB) with sub-second latency and block-level finality.

The chain has two main components:

* **HyperCore**: The matching engine and clearinghouse for perps and spot
* **HyperEVM**: An EVM-compatible environment for additional applications

Bullpen interacts primarily with HyperCore.

## How Orders Execute

1. You place an order through Bullpen
2. Order is signed with your wallet
3. Submitted to Hyperliquid's consensus layer
4. Matched against the onchain orderbook
5. Trade settles in the same block

Every step happens onchain. There's no off-chain matching or hidden orderbook.

## Order Book Mechanics

Hyperliquid uses strict **price-time priority**:

1. Best prices execute first
2. At the same price, oldest orders fill first

Orders must use discrete tick sizes and lot sizes (integer multiples defined per asset).

## Margin System

For perpetuals, Hyperliquid enforces margin at two points:

1. **Order placement**: Checks you have sufficient margin to place the order
2. **Match time**: Verifies margin again before finalizing the trade

This "double check" ensures both parties meet collateral requirements even if price moved between order placement and execution.

## Supported Order Types

Bullpen exposes all Hyperliquid order types:

* Market
* Limit (GTC, IOC, ALO)
* Stop Market / Stop Limit
* Take Profit / Stop Loss
* TWAP
* Scale Orders

See [Order Types](/trading/order-types.md) for details on each.

## Spot vs Perps

Both spot and perps trade on the same infrastructure, but with different settlement:

|            | Perpetuals          | Spot           |
| ---------- | ------------------- | -------------- |
| Settlement | USDC margin account | Token delivery |
| Expiry     | Never               | N/A            |
| Leverage   | Up to 50x           | 1x             |
| Funding    | Hourly              | None           |

## Key Hyperliquid Resources

For deeper technical details:

* [Hyperliquid Docs: About](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs)
* [Order Types](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/order-types)
* [Margining](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/margining)
* [Liquidations](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/liquidations)
* [Oracle & Price Indices](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/robust-price-indices)
* [API Documentation](https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/for-developers/api)
